Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Language Skills Act (Lieutenant Governor of New Brunswick)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Language Skills Act (Lieutenant Governor of New Brunswick)

Provision.Text: This enactment adds the office of Lieutenant Governor of New Brunswick to the list of offices subject to the Language Skills Act.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Paragraph 2(a) of the Language Skills Act is renumbered as paragraph (a.1) and section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following before paragraph (a.1):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Lieutenant Governor of New Brunswick or any other person appointed by the Governor in Council to carry on the government of the province;

Section.Text: Any person appointed to any of the following offices must, at the time of his or her appointment, be able to speak and understand clearly both official languages:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Auditor General of Canada, appointed pursuant to subsection 3(1) of the Auditor General Act;

Subsection.Text: Sections (2) to (4) apply if Bill S-220, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Language Skills Act (Governor General) (in this section referred to as the âother Actâ), receives royal assent.
If Section 1 of the other Act comes into force before section 1 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 1 of this Act is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 2 of the Languages Skills Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.01)
Paragraph.Text: the Lieutenant Governor of New Brunswick or any other person appointed by the Governor in Council to carry on the government of the province;

Subsection.Text: If section 1 of this Act comes into force before section 1 of the other Act, then on the first day on which section 1 of the other Act and section 1 of this Act are in force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 1 of the other Act is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 2(a) of the Language Skills Act is renumbered as paragraph (a.01) and section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following before paragraph (a.01):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Governor General of Canada or any other chief executive officer or administrator carrying on the Government of Canada on behalf and in the name of the Sovereign, by whatever title designated;

Subsection.Text: If section 1 of this Act and section 1 of the other Act come into force on the same day, section 1 of this Act is deemed to have come into force before section 1 of the other Act and subsection (2) applies as a consequence.
